title: What Does an AI PM Actually Do?
description: A friend considering a career switch asks what an AI PM does day to day. Pull from the Cat Wu / Lenny's Podcast discussion.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I'm thinking about applying for AI PM roles. What do they actually do?

templates.text: Less roadmap alignment than a normal PM, more about shrinking the gap from idea to user.
templates.hint_zh: 比一般 PM 少做路線圖對齊,更多是縮短「想法到用戶」的距離。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What's different from a regular product role?

templates.text: You need a sense of where the model is good and where it isn't. That changes every few months.
templates.hint_zh: 你要有感覺模型哪裡好、哪裡不行。這每幾個月都在變。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Do I need a coding background?

templates.text: It really helps. Cat Wu said engineering background lets you judge how hard things actually are.
templates.hint_zh: 幫助很大。Cat Wu 說工程背景能讓你判斷事情實際有多難。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What about PRDs? Do AI PMs still write them?

templates.text: Most teams skip them. They use weekly metrics readouts and team principles instead.
templates.hint_zh: 多數團隊不寫了,改用每週數據報告跟團隊原則替代。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What's the most important skill, then?

templates.text: Product taste. The intuition for what to build and what to drop.
templates.hint_zh: 產品品味。判斷該做什麼、該放掉什麼的直覺。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: How do you even build that?

templates.text: Watch users push the product to its limits. Cat spends thirty percent of her time doing that.
templates.hint_zh: 看用戶把產品推到極限。Cat 自己花三成時間做這件事。
